structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints (ASHRAE 62.1)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: VAV terminal units, fan-powered terminal units, SZVAV AHUs
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Calculates Vmin and Vmin* based on ventilation requirements, with modifications for occupancy, windows, and CO2 sensors.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone requires mechanical ventilation and project complies with ASHRAE Standard 62.1.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Set Vmin = Voz if AHU has been 100% OA for 10 min, else 1.5*Voz if 'AUTO', else as entered. Set Vmin* = Vmin except as modified by occupancy/window/CO2 conditions. For CO2 DCV, use P-only loop to reset Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Vmin shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes;

anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
judge_reason: Extracts a control sequence for calculating minimum outdoor air and airflow setpoints per ASHRAE 62.1, citing specific sections.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Sensor Calibration Alarm for TAV Zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generates Level 3 alarm if CO2 concentration is too low (sensor may be out of calibration) or too high (exceeds setpoint).
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in TAV mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If CO2 < 300 ppm, or zone unoccupied >2 hours and CO2 > 600 ppm, generate Level 3 alarm indicating sensor may be out of calibration. If CO2 exceeds setpoint +10% for >10 minutes, generate Level 3 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: CO2 < 300 ppm OR (unoccupied >2 hours AND CO2 > 600 ppm) OR (CO2 > setpoint +10% for >10 min)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm.
evidence_text: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm.
judge_reason: Captures a specific fault diagnostic rule from Guideline 36 with clear evidence and operational use.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) for Zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Pulse width modulates airflow setpoint when active setpoint is below controllable minimum to meet average ventilation requirements.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than lowest controllable airflow Vm.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Set total cycle time TCT = 15 min (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open, set Vspt* = Vm; during closed, set Vspt* = 0 and disable damper loop. Randomize initial open period using RNDM*OP.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows:

evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows:
evidence_text: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows:
judge_reason: Captures a real control sequence (pulse width modulation for TAV) from section 5.2.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Temperature Setpoint Adjustments and Limits
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All single-zone systems and subzones (VAV boxes, fan-powered boxes, etc.)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Defines occupied/unoccupied setpoints, anti-overlap logic, local adjustment limits, demand limit adjustments, window switch overrides, and occupancy sensor setbacks.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone is in any operating mode.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Prevent heating setpoint > cooling setpoint - 0.5°C (1°F). Limit occupied cooling setpoint to 22-27°C (72-80°F) and heating to 18-22°C (65-72°F). For demand limit, increase cooling setpoint by 0.5/1/2°C (1/2/4°F) for levels 1/2/3; decrease heating similarly. For open window, set heating to 4°C (40°F) and cooling to 49°C (120°F). For occupancy sensor, after 5 min unpopulated, decrease heating by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling by 0.5°C (1°F); restore after 1 min populated.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The software shall prevent the following: a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoints shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).

evidence_quote: The software shall prevent the following: a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoints shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).
evidence_text: The software shall prevent the following: a. The heating setpoint from exceeding the cooling setpoint minus 0.5°C (1°F) (i.e., the minimum difference between heating and cooling setpoints shall be 0.5°C [1°F]).
judge_reason: Captures a real control sequence with setpoint limits and anti-overlap logic from ASHRAE Guideline 36.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Temperature Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generates Level 4 or Level 3 alarms when zone temperature deviates significantly from setpoint for 10 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone temperature exceeds thresholds relative to setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 min: Level 4 alarm. If 3°C (5°F) above for 10 min: Level 3 alarm. Similarly for low temperature: 2°C below heating setpoint for 10 min: Level 4; 3°C below for 10 min: Level 3. Suppress alarms after setpoint change and during warmup/cooldown modes.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temp > cooling setpoint + 2°C for 10 min OR > +3°C for 10 min OR < heating setpoint - 2°C for 10 min OR < -3°C for 10 min
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm.
judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule from Section 5.3.6.1 specifying alarm generation on temperature deviation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Control Loops and Zone State Determination
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Separate PI control loops for heating and cooling, with enable/disable logic based on temperature relative to setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable heating loop when space temp < heating setpoint; disable when temp > setpoint and output zero for 30 sec. Enable cooling loop when space temp > cooling setpoint; disable when temp < setpoint and output zero for 30 sec. Use PI logic; proportional-only not acceptable. Zone state: heating if heating output nonzero and cooling zero; cooling if cooling output nonzero and heating zero; deadband otherwise.
configurable_values: P and I gains (adjustable by operator)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.

evidence_quote: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.
evidence_text: The Heating Loop shall be enabled whenever the space temperature is below the current zone heating set-point temperature and disabled when space temperature is above the current zone heating setpoint temperature and the loop output is zero for 30 seconds.
judge_reason: Captures a real operational sequence from Guideline 36 about zone control loops with enable/disable logic.

structured_payload_candidate.summary: Resets duct static pressure setpoint using trim-and-respond logic based on zone hot-duct static pressure requests.

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

evidence_quote: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.
evidence_text: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU Supply Air Temperature Setpoint Reset
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV Air-Handling Unit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Resets heating supply air temperature setpoint using trim-and-respond logic during Occupied Mode; fixed at Max_HtgSAT during Warmup and Setback.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: During Occupied Mode, use T&R logic with SP0=SPmax, SPmin=21°C (70°F), SPmax=Max_HtgSAT, Td=10 min, T=2 min, I=2, R=zone heating SAT requests, SPtrim=-0.2°C (-0.4°F), SPres=+0.3°C (+0.6°F), SPres-max=+0.8°C (+1.4°F). During Warmup and Setback, set setpoint to Max_HtgSAT.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.

evidence_quote: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.
evidence_text: During Occupied Mode (Table 5.17.2.2). Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) between 21°C (70°F) and Max_HtgSAT.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU AFDD Fault Conditions
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV Air-Handling Unit
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Dual-fan dual-duct heating VAV AHU (heating duct)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Three fault conditions: low duct static pressure with fan at full speed, SAT too low in full heating, and temperature rise across inactive heating coil.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: AHU is operating and not in mode change delay.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Evaluate FC#1: if DSP avg < DSPSP - εDSP and VFD speed ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD, report fault. FC#2: if SAT avg < SATSP - εSAT and HC ≥ 99%, report fault. FC#3: if RAT avg - SAT avg ≥ sqrt(εSAT²+εRAT²) + ΔTSF and HC = 0%, report fault. Use 30 min alarm delay and 30 min mode delay.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: FC#1: DSP < DSPSP - εDSP AND VFDSPD ≥ 99% - εVFDSPD; FC#2: SAT AVG < SATSP - εSAT AND HC ≥ 99%; FC#3: RAT AVG - SAT AVG ≥ sqrt(εSAT²+εRAT²) + ΔTSF AND HC = 0%
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Table 5.17.4.5 DFDD Heating AHU Fault Conditions FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD

evidence_quote: Table 5.17.4.5 DFDD Heating AHU Fault Conditions FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD
evidence_text: Table 5.17.4.5 DFDD Heating AHU Fault Conditions FC#1 Equation DSP < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD
judge_reason: The extraction captures a specific fault diagnostic rule from ASHRAE Guideline 36, including table reference and evidence of equation, which is operational knowledge.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Fan Speed and SAT Setpoint Reset
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Simultaneously resets fan speed and two SAT setpoints (SATsp for economizer/heating, SATsp-C for cooling coil) based on heating/cooling loop signals and outdoor air temperature.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Fan speed: heating loop 100-50%: MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed; 50-0%: MinSpeed; deadband: MinSpeed; cooling loop 0-25%: MinSpeed; 25-50%: MinSpeed to MedSpeed; 50-75%: MedSpeed; 75-100%: MedSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. MedSpeed resets linearly with OAT between endpoints. SATsp: heating loop 100-50%: Heat_SAT; 50-0%: Heat_SAT to deadband value; deadband: deadband value; cooling loop 0-25%: deadband to Cool_SAT-1°C; 25-50%: unchanged; 50-75%: Cool_SAT-1°C; SATsp-C: deadband to Cool_SAT; 75-100%: unchanged. Deadband value = average of zone heating and cooling setpoints, limited to 21-24°C (70-75°F).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.

evidence_quote: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
evidence_text: For a heating-loop signal of 100% to 50%, fan speed is reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinSpeed.
judge_reason: Captures a real control sequence from Guideline 36 Section 5.18.4 with specific operational parameters and reset logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SZVAV AHU Supply Air Temperature Control Loop Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Single-zone VAV AHU with economizer and heating coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Maps SAT control loop output to modulate heating coil and economizer/return dampers in sequence.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Control loop output is mapped: heating coil modulates from 0-100% over a portion of the output range; economizer damper modulates from MinOA-P to MaxOA-P over another portion; return damper is complementary. Provide separate gains for each section.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2.

evidence_quote: Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2.
evidence_text: Supply air temperature shall be controlled to SATsp by a control loop whose output is mapped to sequence the heating coil (if applicable) and economizer dampers as shown in the Figure 5.18.5.2.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SZVAV AHU Minimum Outdoor Air Control without AFMS
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: SZVAV AHU without outdoor airflow measurement station
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Controls minimum OA damper position based on fan speed and MinOAsp using linear interpolation.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate MinPos* and DesPos* as linear interpolation between MinPosMin/Max and DesPosMin/Max based on current fan speed. If MinOAsp=0, set MinOA-P=0. Else set MinOA-P proportional to MinOAsp between MinOA and DesOA.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.

evidence_quote: At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
evidence_text: At least once per minute while the zone is in Occupied Mode, the BAS shall calculate MinPos* as a linear interpolation between MinPosMin and MinPosMax based on the current fan speed.
judge_reason: Captures a real operational sequence from Guideline 36 for minimum OA damper control based on fan speed interpolation.

structured_payload_candidate.title: SZVAV AHU Chilled-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Sends requests to reset CHW temperature based on SAT deviation from setpoint and valve position.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Supply fan is proven ON and cooling is active.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If SAT > SATsp-C + 3°C for 2 min, send 3 requests. Else if SAT > SATsp-C + 2°C for 2 min, send 2 requests. Else if CHW valve > 95%, send 1 request until valve < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.

evidence_quote: If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.
evidence_text: If the supply air temperature exceeds SATsp-C by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes, send 3 requests.
judge_reason: Captures a real control sequence for chilled-water reset in SZVAV AHU.

structured_payload_candidate.title: General Constant Speed Exhaust Fan Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Two options: operate with associated Zone Groups, or cycle to maintain room temperature.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Option a: Fan operates when any associated supply fan is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Option b: Fan runs when zone temperature rises above active cooling setpoint until temperature falls more than 1°C (2°F) below setpoint for 2 minutes.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode.

evidence_quote: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode.
evidence_text: Exhaust fan shall operate when any of the associated system supply fans is proven on and any associated Zone Group is in the Occupied Mode.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Coil Unit Supply Fan Speed and SAT Control
